title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.1
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
keywords: not, secrets, credentials, sec.1, hardcode, long-lived, secret-like, literals, should, live, secret, management
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.

title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.2
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
keywords: shell, not, command, sec.2, build, commands, trusted, primitives, concatenated, strings, execution, paths
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.

title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.3
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
keywords: text, sql, query, should, sec.3, parameterize, instead, composing, dynamically, built, variables, difficult
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: sql_injection, dynamic_sql
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.

title: Do not leave fallback secrets or test credentials in production code paths
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
summary: Backup credentials often become the least-audited credential in the system.
embedding_text:

language_id: shared
context_id:

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.

title: Keep command execution APIs structured so the executable and arguments remain distinct
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.
summary: Security review is much stronger when command construction never depends on ad hoc shell parsing.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured process APIs, validate each argument according to its contract, and avoid passing shell fragments through string concatenation.
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.
fix_guidance: Use structured process APIs, validate each argument according to its contract, and avoid passing shell fragments through string concatenation.

title: Dynamic query structure should stay allow-listed even when values are parameterized
text: ORDER BY fragments, table identifiers, and optional clauses can still create injection or correctness risk when the allowed structures are not tightly bounded.
summary: Parameter binding protects values, not uncontrolled structural SQL changes.

fix_guidance: Keep structural variation behind a small allow-list and continue binding values separately from the SQL text.

title: Example, bootstrap, and fallback credentials in committed code should be treated as real secrets unless clearly impossible to activate
text: Backup and bootstrap secrets often become the least-audited authentication path in a system because everyone assumes they are temporary or unreachable.
summary: "Test" or "dev" labels do not remove rotation, misuse, or confusion risk once credentials are committed.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove committed fallback credentials or move them into an isolated local-only development path that cannot ship as a production default.
text: Backup and bootstrap secrets often become the least-audited authentication path in a system because everyone assumes they are temporary or unreachable.
fix_guidance: Remove committed fallback credentials or move them into an isolated local-only development path that cannot ship as a production default.

title: Generic image references should avoid floating latest tags in reviewable config
text: A generic YAML file that carries an image reference is easier to operate when the referenced version is explicit and can be traced back during rollback or incident analysis.
summary: Mutable image tags weaken reproducibility even outside dedicated deployment manifests.

category: configuration
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: latest_tag
fix_guidance: Replace latest with an explicit version or digest that matches the release process for the referenced image.
text: A generic YAML file that carries an image reference is easier to operate when the referenced version is explicit and can be traced back during rollback or incident analysis.
fix_guidance: Replace latest with an explicit version or digest that matches the release process for the referenced image.

title: Avoid privileged containers unless the workload truly requires host-level access
text: Privileged containers bypass many of the normal isolation expectations around a Kubernetes workload and should be exceptional.
summary: `privileged: true` expands the container trust boundary dramatically.

pack_id: kubernetes_yaml

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: privileged_true
fix_guidance: Drop privilege escalation or isolate the workload behind the narrowest capability set it actually needs.
text: Privileged containers bypass many of the normal isolation expectations around a Kubernetes workload and should be exceptional.
fix_guidance: Drop privilege escalation or isolate the workload behind the narrowest capability set it actually needs.

title: Avoid running application containers as root by default
text: A root runtime identity increases the blast radius of an application compromise and makes least-privilege reasoning harder.
summary: Root in a manifest should be a conscious exception, not the baseline.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: run_as_root
fix_guidance: Configure a non-root user and align file ownership or init behavior so the workload does not depend on UID 0.
text: A root runtime identity increases the blast radius of an application compromise and makes least-privilege reasoning harder.
fix_guidance: Configure a non-root user and align file ownership or init behavior so the workload does not depend on UID 0.

title: Privileged pods should justify the exact host capability boundary they require
text: Kubernetes manifests are easier to secure when a workload asks only for the capabilities it truly needs instead of inheriting near-host-level privilege.
summary: privileged true is rarely the smallest permission set that solves the workload need.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: privileged_true
fix_guidance: Replace privileged mode with the smallest set of capabilities, mounts, or node-level isolation the workload actually requires.
text: Kubernetes manifests are easier to secure when a workload asks only for the capabilities it truly needs instead of inheriting near-host-level privilege.
fix_guidance: Replace privileged mode with the smallest set of capabilities, mounts, or node-level isolation the workload actually requires.

title: Root runtime identity should be an exception with an explicit deployment rationale
text: A manifest that sets runAsUser 0 makes the workload harder to contain after compromise and deserves a clear justification or a redesign toward non-root execution.
summary: Running as UID 0 should stand out as an intentional security tradeoff.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: run_as_root
fix_guidance: Prefer a non-root user and adjust init, volume ownership, or image build steps so the container no longer requires UID 0.
text: A manifest that sets runAsUser 0 makes the workload harder to contain after compromise and deserves a clear justification or a redesign toward non-root execution.
fix_guidance: Prefer a non-root user and adjust init, volume ownership, or image build steps so the container no longer requires UID 0.

title: allowPrivilegeEscalation should be exceptional and tied to a narrowly justified workload need
text: A manifest that enables privilege escalation deserves the same least-privilege scrutiny as other securityContext exceptions because it changes what post-compromise execution can do.
summary: Privilege escalation widens the runtime attack surface even when the container is not fully privileged.

title: hostNetwork should be treated as a strong isolation-boundary exception
text: `hostNetwork: true` should stand out as an architectural exception because it couples pod behavior to node networking and reduces the normal isolation reviewers expect from Kubernetes.
summary: Sharing the node network namespace changes the workload's blast radius and operational assumptions.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: host_network_true
fix_guidance: Prefer ordinary pod networking unless host-level networking is explicitly required and documented as part of the workload contract.
text: `hostNetwork: true` should stand out as an architectural exception because it couples pod behavior to node networking and reduces the normal isolation reviewers expect from Kubernetes.
fix_guidance: Prefer ordinary pod networking unless host-level networking is explicitly required and documented as part of the workload contract.

title: Kubernetes workload images should avoid floating latest tags
text: A Kubernetes manifest is part of the deployment contract for a workload. Floating `latest` image tags weaken rollback, incident reconstruction, and release explainability because the same manifest revision can point at materially different container contents over time.
summary: Mutable workload image tags make rollout provenance drift even when the manifest itself is unchanged.
embedding_text:
rule_no: YAML.K8S.7
pack_id: kubernetes_yaml
language_id: yaml
context_id: kubernetes
dialect_id: 
section: YAMLK8S
title: Kubernetes workload images should avoid floating latest tags
summary: Mutable workload image tags make rollout provenance drift even when the manifest itself is unchanged.
keywords: tags, manifest, kubernetes, workload, floating, latest, image, yaml.k8s.7, images, should, avoid, mutable
category: configuration
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: latest_tag
fix_guidance: Pin the workload image to an explicit version or digest so the rollout input remains traceable and reproducible.
text: A Kubernetes manifest is part of the deployment contract for a workload. Floating `latest` image tags weaken rollback, incident reconstruction, and release explainability because the same manifest revision can point at materially different container contents over time.
fix_guidance: Pin the workload image to an explicit version or digest so the rollout input remains traceable and reproducible.

title: Avoid broad workflow token permissions when narrower scopes are sufficient

fix_guidance: Declare only the permissions the workflow step needs and keep write scopes limited to the jobs that truly require them.

title: Broad workflow token permissions should be scoped to the smallest set of jobs possible
text: CI jobs vary in privilege needs. Review workflow permissions so elevated scopes appear only on the jobs that truly require them instead of the whole pipeline.
summary: Defaulting the entire pipeline to write-all expands the blast radius of one compromised step.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: permissions_write_all
fix_guidance: Move elevated permissions to the narrowest workflow or job scope that still supports the required action.
text: CI jobs vary in privilege needs. Review workflow permissions so elevated scopes appear only on the jobs that truly require them instead of the whole pipeline.
fix_guidance: Move elevated permissions to the narrowest workflow or job scope that still supports the required action.

title: Mutable CI runtime images should be treated as provenance and rollback risk
text: A floating execution image can change toolchains, package sets, and dependency graphs without a repository diff, which complicates debugging and incident response.
summary: latest-like tags make it harder to explain why the same workflow changed behavior.

fix_guidance: Pin the image or action revision tightly enough that the workflow environment remains traceable and repeatable.

title: Branch-ref GitHub Actions should be treated as mutable execution dependencies
text: CI action references are part of the build's executable supply chain. Branch refs weaken provenance, rollback, and incident reconstruction compared to tagged or SHA-pinned refs.
summary: `@main` or `@master` makes workflow behavior drift without a repository change in the caller.

category: security

trigger_patterns: uses_branch_ref
fix_guidance: Pin actions to a vetted immutable ref, ideally a commit SHA or another organization-approved immutable release boundary.
text: CI action references are part of the build's executable supply chain. Branch refs weaken provenance, rollback, and incident reconstruction compared to tagged or SHA-pinned refs.
fix_guidance: Pin actions to a vetted immutable ref, ideally a commit SHA or another organization-approved immutable release boundary.

title: CI jobs should not pipe remote downloads directly into shell interpreters
text: A pipeline step that downloads text from the network and immediately executes it as a shell script weakens provenance review and makes reruns harder to explain safely.
summary: CI bootstrap should keep fetch, verification, and execution as distinct trust steps.

text: A pipeline step that downloads text from the network and immediately executes it as a shell script weakens provenance review and makes reruns harder to explain safely.
fix_guidance: Split the flow into fetch, verify, and execute stages or replace it with a pinned artifact and explicit checksum or signature verification step.

title: Insecure artifact downloads in CI should be exceptional and paired with explicit verification
text: CI pipelines often become part of the deployment chain. Using insecure download flags in that environment expands the artifact trust boundary and should only happen with an explicit verification story.

fix_guidance: Keep TLS verification enabled or add an explicit pinned checksum or signature verification path before the artifact is used.

title: CI service and helper images should avoid floating latest tags
text: Helper containers and service images are part of the executable CI environment. Floating latest tags make test and deploy behavior harder to reproduce when debugging or rolling back.
summary: Mutable service containers make pipeline behavior drift even when the checked-in job steps do not change.

title: pull_request_target workflows should make privileged PR execution boundaries explicit
text: In GitHub Actions, pull_request_target runs in the base repository context, not the untrusted fork context. That can be valid for carefully isolated workflows, but it should not be introduced without an obvious secret, token, and checkout safety story.
summary: pull_request_target changes the trust model for forked PR automation and can expose secrets or write scopes unexpectedly.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: github_actions_pull_request_target
fix_guidance: Prefer pull_request for untrusted PR execution, or isolate pull_request_target jobs so they never run untrusted code with privileged tokens or secrets.
text: In GitHub Actions, pull_request_target runs in the base repository context, not the untrusted fork context. That can be valid for carefully isolated workflows, but it should not be introduced without an obvious secret, token, and checkout safety story.
fix_guidance: Prefer pull_request for untrusted PR execution, or isolate pull_request_target jobs so they never run untrusted code with privileged tokens or secrets.

title: Product config should not commit secret-like literal values directly
text: Secrets committed as plain YAML values widen the blast radius of repository access and make rotation or environment separation harder to reason about.

fix_guidance: Move secret material to a dedicated secret manager or environment-specific injection path and keep only the reference in committed config.

title: additionalProperties: true should be a deliberate schema-boundary decision
text: A schema that accepts any undeclared key is harder to validate mentally and weakens the ability to catch accidental producer drift early.
summary: Allowing arbitrary extra keys broadens the contract and reduces drift detection.
embedding_text:
rule_no: YAML.SCHEMA.1
pack_id: schema_yaml

title: additionalProperties: true should be a deliberate schema-boundary decision
summary: Allowing arbitrary extra keys broadens the contract and reduces drift detection.
keywords: drift, yaml.schema.1, additionalproperties:, true, should, deliberate, schema-boundary, decision, allowing, arbitrary, extra, keys
category: configuration
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: yaml_additional_properties_true
fix_guidance: Prefer a closed or narrowly constrained schema unless the extension boundary is explicitly part of the contract.
text: A schema that accepts any undeclared key is harder to validate mentally and weakens the ability to catch accidental producer drift early.

title: Boolean-like schema defaults should be explicit instead of ambiguous YAML words
text: Schema defaults act as durable documentation for producers and consumers. Ambiguous YAML words increase the chance that tooling, humans, or downstream formats disagree about the intended type.
summary: Bare on/off/yes/no defaults make cross-parser intent and documentation harder to trust.
embedding_text:
rule_no: YAML.SCHEMA.2
pack_id: schema_yaml
language_id: yaml
context_id: schema_config
dialect_id: 
section: YAMLSCHEMA
title: Boolean-like schema defaults should be explicit instead of ambiguous YAML words
summary: Bare on/off/yes/no defaults make cross-parser intent and documentation harder to trust.
keywords: defaults, schema, ambiguous, yaml, words, documentation, yaml.schema.2, boolean-like, should, explicit, instead, bare
category: configuration
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: yaml_ambiguous_boolean_default
fix_guidance: Use an explicit boolean or a quoted string that matches the actual schema type so the default contract is unambiguous.
